description_long: This object is a military aircraft, specifically a World War II-era twin-engine bomber with a dark olive drab exterior. The fuselage is streamlined with a glass nose section, housing a cockpit and bombardier station, and features a dorsal turret and tail gun position. The wings are mid-mounted and display roundel insignia, while the tail section is equipped with a twin-fin and rudder assembly. The aircraft has retractable landing gear, visible in the down position, and propellers on each engine nacelle. Markings and insignia, including roundels and alphanumeric codes, are painted on the fuselage and wings, indicating its use by the Royal Air Force. The overall design is robust, with multiple windows and gun emplacements for defensive purposes.
description: A World War II-era twin-engine military bomber with olive drab paint, glass nose, and RAF insignia. Features include a dorsal turret, tail gun, and mid-mounted wings with roundels.
description_view_0: Side view showing the glass nose, cockpit, dorsal turret, and RAF markings on the fuselage and wings.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the twin tail fins, tail gun position, and the symmetry of the wings and engine nacelles.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the propellers, glass nose, and additional fuselage markings.
description_view_3: Front view emphasizing the glass cockpit, propellers, and the aircraft's wide wingspan.
